paper_title: Bayesian Divergence-Time Estimation with Genome-Wide Single-Nucleotide Polymorphism Data of Sea Catfishes (Ariidae) Supports Miocene Closure of the Panamanian Isthmus

methods.method_name: Bayesian Divergence-Time Estimation with Simulated SNP Data
methods.description: This method involves designing experiments based on simulated SNP data to test the performance of the MSC model implemented in SNAPP for divergence-time estimation. It assesses various factors such as data set size, node-age constraints, population sizes, and sampling strategies.

steps.description: Test the accuracy and precision of divergence times estimated with SNAPP and the degree to which these are influenced by the size of the SNP data set and the placement of node-age constraints.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate SNAPP's divergence-time estimation accuracy with varying SNP data set sizes and node-age constraints

steps.description: Assess the effect of larger population sizes on divergence-time estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze impact of population size on divergence-time estimates using SNAPP

steps.description: Test how the precision of estimates depends on the number of individuals sampled per species.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine effect of sample size on precision of divergence-time estimates

steps.description: Evaluate SNAPP's estimates of divergence times, the molecular clock rate, and the population size, based on data sets that include or exclude invariant sites, with or without ascertainment-bias correction.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess SNAPP's divergence-time estimates with varying data set conditions

steps.description: Compare divergence-time estimates based on the MSC model implemented in SNAPP with those inferred with concatenated data using BEAST.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare SNAPP and BEAST divergence-time estimates

methods.description: This method involves simulating genome-wide SNP data using parameters similar to empirical analyses with SNAPP, including species tree generation, gene tree simulation, and sequence evolution modeling.

steps.description: Generate 100 species trees with the pure-birth Yule process, conditioned to have 20 extant species.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate species trees using Yule process

steps.description: Simulate 10,000 gene trees for each species tree using DendroPy, with constant and equal population sizes for all branches.
Simulate sequences along each gene tree with Seq-Gen according to the Jukes-Cantor model of sequence evolution.
steps.searchable_summary: Simulate sequences using Seq-Gen and Jukes-Cantor model

steps.description: Select a single SNP at random from all except completely invariable alignments to generate data sets of close to 10,000 unlinked SNPs.
steps.searchable_summary: Select SNPs from alignments to create SNP data sets

steps.description: Subsample SNP data sets to generate sets of 300, 1000, and 3000 bi-allelic SNPs for each species tree.
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample SNP data sets to specific sizes

methods.method_name: Inferring Divergence Times from Simulated SNP Data
methods.description: This method involves using SNAPP to infer divergence times from simulated SNP data, with specific modifications to standard analysis settings to accommodate the data.

steps.description: Fix forward and reverse mutation-rate parameters to 1.0 to assume a symmetric substitution model.
steps.searchable_summary: Set mutation-rate parameters for symmetric substitution model

steps.description: Add a parameter for the rate of a strict molecular clock and use the one-on-x prior for the clock rate.
steps.searchable_summary: Add strict molecular clock parameter with one-on-x prior

steps.description: Calibrate the molecular clock rate through age constraints on a single node of the species tree.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate clock rate with node age constraints

steps.description: Estimate a single value of population size for all branches, assuming equal population sizes.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate uniform population size for all branches

steps.description: Fix the species-tree topology to the true topology for divergence-time estimation.
